{{Short description|British academic}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=June 2017}}
{{Use British English|date=March 2021}}
'''Carol Elspeth Goodeve Brayne''' [[Order of the British Empire|CBE]] is a British [[Epidemiology|epidemiologist]], [[Professor|academic]], [[Medical doctor|physician]] and [[Medical research|medical researcher]]. Her current position is Professor Emeritus and Senior Visiting Fellow in the Department of Psychiatry at [[University of Cambridge]].

==Early life, family and education==
Brayne received a degree in medicine from the [[Royal Free Hospital School of Medicine]], [[University of London]],<ref name= archive2019-cambridge-profile>{{cite web|url=http://www.phpc.cam.ac.uk/people/phab/phab-senior-academic-staff/professor-carol-brayne/ |title=Professor Carol Brayne | publisher= Department of Public Health and Primary Care, [[University of Cambridge]] | website= phpc.cam.ac.uk |date= | archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20190501222205/https://www.phpc.cam.ac.uk/people/phab/phab-senior-academic-staff/professor-carol-brayne/| archive-date= May 1, 2019|access-date=28 June 2017}}</ref> in 1981.<ref name= lancet-profile>{{cite journal| first= Stephen| last= Pincock| journal= [[The Lancet]]| url= https://www.thelancet.com/pdfs/journals/lancet/PIIS0140673608610570.pdf| title= Carol Brayne: studying dementia in the brain and in populations| date= July 19, 2008 | volume= 372| issue= 9634| page= 197| doi= 10.1016/S0140-6736(08)61057-0| pmid= 18640444| access-date= February 4, 2025}}</ref> She subsequently earned a masters of science in epidemiology.<ref name= cambridge-profile>{{cite web|url= https://www.cph.cam.ac.uk/staff/carol-brayne |title= Carol Brayne | publisher= Cambridge Public Health, University of Cambridge | website= cpc.cam.ac.uk |date= 12 December 2022|access-date= 4 February 2025}}</ref>

==Career==
Brayne was the Professor of Public Health Medicine at the University of Cambridge from 2001 to 2024.<ref name= cambridge-profile /> She joined Cambridge in 1985.<ref name= cambridge-profile /> She has been Chair of Wellcome's Population and Public Health Review Group.<ref name="wellcome1">{{cite web|url=https://wellcome.ac.uk/news/awards-wellcome-community-queens-birthday-honours |title=Awards for Wellcome community in Queen's Birthday Honours |publisher=Wellcome | website= wellcome.ac.uk |date=19 June 2017 |access-date=28 June 2017}}</ref> She was Director of the [[Cambridge Institute of Public Health]].<ref name= cambridge-profile /> She has been a special advisor for the [[Royal College of Physicians]] and a senior investigator at the [[National Institute for Health Research]] (NIHR).<ref name= archive2019-cambridge-profile />

Brayne has worked for the [[National Health Service]].<ref name= lancet-profile /> She also spent six months as a visiting fellow in Australia in 1997.<ref name= lancet-profile />

==Honors and awards==
In the [[2017 Birthday Honours]],<ref name= cambridge-profile /> Brayne was appointed [[Order of the British Empire|CBE]], "[f]or services to Public Health Medicine".<ref name="wellcome1"/><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.itv.com/news/anglia/2017-06-16/cambridge-scientists-among-those-honoured-by-queen/ |title=Cambridge scientists among those honoured by Queen &#124; Anglia |publisher= ITV News | website= itv.com |date=2017-06-16 |access-date=2017-06-28}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Brayne and her husband have raised four children.<ref name= lancet-profile />

==Selected publications==
*{{cite journal| title= Age-Associated White Matter Lesions: The MRC Cognitive Function and Ageing Study| vauthors= Wharton SB, Simpson JE, Brayne C, Ince PG| journal= Brain Pathol| display-authors= 3| date= January 2015| volume= 25| number= 1| pages= 35–43| doi= 10.1111/bpa.12219| pmid= 25521175| pmc= 8029351}}
*{{cite journal| title= Potential for primary prevention of Alzheimer's disease: an analysis of population-based data| vauthors= Norton S, Matthews FE, Barnes DE, Yaffe K, Brayne C | display-authors= 3| journal= Lancet Neurol| date= August 2014 | volume= 13| number= 8| pages= 788–94| doi= 10.1016/S1474-4422(14)70136-X | pmid= 25030513 | url= https://kclpure.kcl.ac.uk/ws/files/13136570/ADPAR_v4_1_TLNrevisedclean.pdf }}
*{{cite journal| title= Adapting to dementia in society: a challenge for our lifetimes and a charge for public health| vauthors= D'Alton S, Hunter S, Whitehouse P, Brayne C, George D| display-authors= 3| journal= J Alzheimers Dis| date=  2014| volume= 42| number= 4| pages= 1151–63| doi= 10.3233/JAD-140213| pmid= 25024316}}
*{{cite journal| title= Dementia prediction for people with stroke in populations: is mild cognitive impairment a useful concept?| vauthors= Stephan BC, Minett T, Muniz Terrera G, Matthews FE, Brayne C| display-authors= 3| journal= Age Ageing| date= January 2015| volume= 44| number= 1| pages= 78–83| doi= 10.1093/ageing/afu085| pmid= 25002454| pmc= 4255614}}
*{{cite journal| title= Reporting standards for studies of diagnostic test accuracy in dementia: The STARDdem Initiative| vauthors= Noel-Storr AH, McCleery JM, Richard E, Ritchie CW, Flicker L, Cullum SJ, Davis D, Quinn TJ, Hyde C, Rutjes AW, Smailagic N, Marcus S, Black S, Blennow K, Brayne C, Fiorivanti M, Johnson JK, Köpke S, Schneider LS, Simmons A, Mattsson N, Zetterberg H, Bossuyt PM, Wilcock G, McShane R| display-authors= 3| journal= [[Neurology (journal)|Neurology]]| date= July 2014 |volume= 83| number= 4| pages= 364–73| doi= 10.1212/WNL.0000000000000621| pmid= 24944261| pmc= 4115600}}
*{{cite journal| title= Gene-wide analysis detects two new susceptibility genes for Alzheimer's disease| vauthors= Escott-Price V, Bellenguez C, Wang LS, Brayne C, Amouyel P, Williams J| display-authors= 3 | journal= PLOS ONE| date= June 12, 2014| volume= 9| number= 6| article-number= e94661| doi= 10.1371/journal.pone.0094661| doi-access= free| pmid= 24922517| pmc= 4055488| bibcode= 2014PLoSO...994661E}}
*{{cite journal| title= DNA damage response and senescence in endothelial cells of human cerebral cortex and relation to Alzheimer's neuropathology progression: a population-based study in the Medical Research Council Cognitive Function and Ageing Study (MRC-CFAS) cohort| vauthors= Garwood CJ, Simpson JE, Al Mashhadi S, Axe C, Wilson S, Heath PR, Shaw PJ, Matthews FE, Brayne C, Ince PG, Wharton SB| display-authors= 3 | journal= Neuropathol Appl Neurobiol| date= December 2014| volume= 40| number= 7| pages= 802–14| doi= 10.1111/nan.12156| pmid= 24861546}}
*{{cite journal| title= Brain haemosiderin in older people: pathological evidence for an ischaemic origin of magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) microbleeds| vauthors= Janaway BM, Simpson JE, Hoggard N, Highley JR, Forster G, Drew D, Gebril OH, Matthews FE, Brayne C, Wharton SB, Ince PG | journal= Neuropathol Appl Neurobiol| display-authors= 3| date= April 2014 | volume= 40| number= 3| pages= 258–69| doi= 10.1111/nan.12062| pmid= 23678850| pmc= 4282337}}
*{{cite journal| title= Health economic evaluation of treatments for Alzheimer's disease: impact of new diagnostic criteria |vauthors= Wimo A, Ballard C, Brayne C, Gauthier S, Handels R, Jones RW, Jonsson L, Khachaturian AS, Kramberger M| display-authors= 3| journal= J Intern Med | date= March 2014 |volume= 275| number= 3| pages= 304–16|doi= 10.1111/joim.12167|pmid= 24605810|url= https://cris.maastrichtuniversity.nl/en/publications/070b56e1-b5b6-4907-a5bf-89d3df56d28d}}
*{{cite journal| title= Health-related quality of life in the Cambridge City over-75s Cohort (CC75C): development of a dementia-specific scale and descriptive analyses| display-authors= 3| vauthors= Perales J, Cosco TD, Stephan BC, Fleming J, Martin S, Haro JM, Brayne C | journal= BMC Geriatr| date= February 2014| volume= 10| number= 14| page= 18| doi= 10.1186/1471-2318-14-18| doi-access= free| pmid= 24512291| pmc= 3922243}}
*{{cite journal| title= C-reactive protein, APOE genotype and longitudinal cognitive change in an older population| vauthors= Lima TA, Adler AL, Minett T, Matthews FE, Brayne C, Marioni RE| display-authors= 3 | journal= Age Ageing| date= March 2014| volume= 43| number= 2| pages= 289–92| doi= 10.1093/ageing/aft193| pmid= 24305621| pmc= 3927773}}
*{{cite journal| title= Cardiovascular disease contributes to Alzheimer's disease: evidence from large-scale genome-wide association studies| vauthors= Liu G, Yao L, Liu J, Jiang Y, Ma G, Chen Z, Zhao B, Li K| display-authors= 3 | journal= Neurobiology of Aging| date= April 2014| volume= 35| number= 4| pages= 786–92| doi= 10.1016/j.neurobiolaging.2013.10.084| pmid= 24231519}}
*{{cite journal| title= COSMIC (Cohort Studies of Memory in an International Consortium): an international consortium to identify risk and protective factors and biomarkers of cognitive ageing and dementia in diverse ethnic and sociocultural groups| vauthors= Sachdev PS, Lipnicki DM, Kochan NA, Crawford JD, Rockwood K, Xiao S, Li J, Li X, Brayne C, Matthews FE, Stephan BC, Lipton RB, Katz MJ, Ritchie K, Carrière I, Ancelin ML, Seshadri S, Au R, Beiser AS, Lam LC, Wong CH, Fung AW, Kim KW, Han JW, Kim TH, Petersen RC, Roberts RO, Mielke MM, Ganguli M, Dodge HH, Hughes T, Anstey KJ, Cherbuin N, Butterworth P, Ng TP, Gao Q, Reppermund S, Brodaty H, Meguro K, Schupf N, Manly J, [[Yaakov Stern|Stern Y]], Lobo A, Lopez-Anton R, Santabárbara J| journal= BMC Neurol| display-authors= 3| date= November 2013 | volume= 6| number= 13| page= 165| doi= 10.1186/1471-2377-13-165| doi-access= free| pmid= 24195705| pmc= 3827845}}
*{{cite journal| title= Meta-analysis of 74,046 individuals identifies 11 new susceptibility loci for Alzheimer's disease| display-authors= 3| vauthors= Lambert JC, Ibrahim-Verbaas CA, Harold D, Brayne C, Williams J, Schellenberg GD, Amouyel P| journal= Nat Genet| date= December 2013 |volume= 45| number= 12| pages= 1452–8| doi= 10.1038/ng.2802| pmid= 24162737| pmc= 3896259}}
